payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Task claiming process performed by children
payload.justification: Signal establishes claiming of completed tasks as a core strategic process children perform.

payload.description: Task and reward establishment process performed by parents
payload.justification: Signal describes parental process of establishing tasks with monetary reward values.

payload.description: Pocket money disbursement process performed by parents
payload.justification: Signal identifies disbursement as strategic process facilitated by reporting visibility.

payload.description: Visibility provision function for task status and availability
payload.justification: Signal describes strategic function of providing children visibility into task information.

payload.description: Earnings totals display function for financial awareness
payload.justification: Signal articulates function of showing weekly earnings to support financial awareness goal.

payload.description: Historical record maintenance function for accountability
payload.justification: Signal identifies maintenance of historical records as strategic function supporting tracking and accountability.

payload.description: Child as strategic participant in pocket money system
payload.justification: Signals identify children as central entities who claim tasks, view available/completed tasks, and see earnings totals.

payload.description: Parent as strategic participant in pocket money system
payload.justification: Signals identify parents as entities who establish rewards, need reporting visibility, and facilitate disbursement.

payload.description: Pocket money task as work unit of strategic importance
payload.justification: Tasks are the core entity children complete, parents establish with monetary values, and children view for awareness.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total as strategic financial metric
payload.justification: Signal identifies weekly earnings totals as distinct entity shown to children for financial awareness.

payload.description: Historical record as strategic accountability artifact
payload.justification: Signal establishes historical records as entities maintained for tracking and accountability purposes.

payload.description: Monetary reward amount as task attribute
payload.justification: Signal describes specific monetary amounts as attributes parents establish for tasks.

payload.description: Task completion status as strategic state indicator
payload.justification: Signals reference completed versus available tasks, indicating completion status as key strategic attribute.

payload.description: Weekly cycle for task claiming and earnings accumulation
payload.justification: Signals reference task claiming throughout the week and weekly earnings totals, establishing weekly cycle as strategic timing.

payload.description: Child role as task claimant and earnings viewer
payload.justification: Signals establish child as strategic role performing task claiming, viewing tasks, and seeing earnings.

payload.description: Parent role as reward definer and disbursement facilitator
payload.justification: Signals define parent role as establishing rewards and using reporting for disbursement facilitation.

payload.description: Establish task-based monetary reward system enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Signal explicitly establishes core purpose of enabling task claiming by children. | Signal defines parental motivation to create system linking tasks to monetary rewards.

payload.description: Provide children task visibility for awareness
payload.justification: Signal expresses goal of giving children awareness through task visibility.

payload.description: Build children's financial awareness through earnings visibility
payload.justification: Signal articulates purpose of showing earnings totals to develop financial awareness.

payload.description: Facilitate accurate pocket money disbursement through reporting
payload.justification: Signal establishes need for reporting visibility to achieve accurate disbursement outcome.

payload.description: Maintain historical accountability through record-keeping
payload.justification: Signal identifies goal of keeping historical records for tracking and accountability purposes.

payload.description: Claim pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Core business process enabling children to claim available tasks for completion within weekly cycles.

payload.description: Define tasks
payload.justification: Business process allowing parents to create task entities with associated monetary values.

payload.description: View available and completed tasks
payload.justification: Business process enabling children to review task status within the current week context.

payload.description: Calculate weekly earnings
payload.justification: Business process aggregating completed task values to produce weekly earnings totals for children.

payload.description: View consolidated completion reports
payload.justification: Business process enabling parents to review child task completions and earned amounts for payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Weekly task reset cycle
payload.justification: Business rule mandating that all task entities return to available state at weekly period boundaries.

payload.description: Historical data retention requirement
payload.justification: Business rule requiring that historical task completion data be retained and remain accessible beyond current operational periods.

payload.description: Task
payload.justification: Core business entity representing work items that can be defined, claimed, completed, and reset within the pocket money system.

payload.description: Child

payload.description: Parent

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Participant Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining and maintaining the roles, relationships, and responsibilities of family members who participate in the household economic system, establishing clear boundaries of authority and entitlement.

payload.name: Work-Reward Obligation Framework
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to establishing and governing the relationship between defined work units and their corresponding monetary entitlements, including the rules for defining, claiming, and fulfilling these obligations.

payload.name: Financial Disbursement Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility for accurate calculation, transparent reporting, and timely transfer of earned monetary entitlements according to established performance records and periodic settlement cycles.

payload.name: Transparency and Information Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to provide participants with visibility into available opportunities, current status of commitments, and financial positions to enable informed participation and decision-making within the system.

payload.name: Historical Record Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise duty to maintain persistent, auditable records of all completed obligations and financial transactions, ensuring long-term accountability, dispute resolution capability, and institutional memory.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, governing, and maintaining the lifecycle of work obligations that children may undertake for compensation, including establishment of value, completion criteria, and recurring availability patterns.

payload.name: Child Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for enabling children to engage with available work obligations, claim those they will perform, execute them, and establish their entitlement to compensation through demonstrated completion.

payload.name: Earnings Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for determining, accumulating, and reporting the monetary obligations owed to participants based on their completed work within defined temporal boundaries, enabling settlement fulfillment.

payload.name: Parental Oversight
payload.description: Business responsibility for providing visibility into child participation patterns, completion achievements, and fulfillment obligations to enable informed parental governance and payment execution.

payload.name: Historical Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving evidence of past task definitions, participation events, and completion outcomes across temporal boundaries to support accountability, pattern analysis, and governance continuity.

payload.name: Temporal Aggregation Logic
payload.description: Logical derivation behavior that aggregates earnings data within weekly temporal boundaries. Defines how earnings values are logically accumulated and bounded by time cycles without prescribing calculation algorithms or reporting mechanisms.

payload.name: Task Lifecycle State Model
payload.description: Logical state transitions governing task availability and the temporal retention policy for historical task records. Defines how tasks move through availability states and when historical data is logically preserved, triggered by weekly cycle boundaries.

payload.name: Task Completion Entity Model
payload.description: Logical structure defining the core completion entity and its associated child entities, including the identity, attributes, and containment relationships that represent a task completion occurrence within the system's logical boundary.

payload.name: Completion History Association Model
payload.description: Logical relationship model governing how completion occurrences are linked to their historical counterparts and task references, defining the traceability structure between active and archived completion records.

payload.name: Platform Compatibility Constraints
payload.description: Physical operating system version requirements and compatibility boundaries that govern deployment target selection and build configuration decisions.

payload.name: Task Execution Interface Specification
payload.description: Detailed specification of child actor task interaction interface including visibility rules, claiming operations, completion event handling, and task state presentation during operational execution cycles.

payload.name: Parent Control Interface Specification
payload.description: Detailed specification of parent actor interface for task definition, monetary assignment operations, earnings report viewing, and payment distribution execution functionality.

payload.name: Multi-Platform Deployment Configuration
payload.description: Precise platform-specific deployment specifications for iOS mobile, Android mobile, and Windows desktop target environments including node configurations and version requirements.

payload.name: Weekly Cycle Timing Specification
payload.description: Detailed timing specifications for weekly operational cycle including task availability reset timing, end-of-period event sequencing, and earnings viewability trigger parameters.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants in the household economic system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the Child entity as a structural participant within the enterprise scope, providing the foundation for role-based obligations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parents as participants in the household economic system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the Parent entity as a structural participant within the enterprise scope, providing the foundation for role-based obligations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide the Child role with task claiming capability, derived from the role definition as task claimant.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view earnings.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide the Child role with earnings viewing capability, derived from the role definition as earnings viewer.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define rewards.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide the Parent role with reward definition capability, derived from the role definition as reward definer.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall facilitate disbursement of rewards.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to facilitate disbursement, derived from the role definition of Parent as disbursement facilitator.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish tasks as work units.
payload.rationale: Entity definition at enterprise scope — task is the work unit the enterprise recognises.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish monetary reward amounts as attributes of tasks.
payload.rationale: Structural relationship between task entity and its monetary attribute at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task completion status.
payload.rationale: Task completion status is a strategic state attribute the enterprise must track.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: How-column process commitment — enterprise enables the claiming capability without prescribing system implementation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to establish tasks.
payload.rationale: How-column process commitment — enterprise enables parent capability to define work units.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to establish rewards.
payload.rationale: How-column process commitment — enterprise enables parent capability to define reward units.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a task-based monetary reward system enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal expressing the governing commitment — establishing the system is the enterprise-level policy constraint.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly settlement cycle for task claiming.
payload.rationale: Defines the temporal governance constraint for the task claiming cycle at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly settlement cycle for earnings accumulation.
payload.rationale: Defines the temporal governance constraint for the earnings accumulation cycle at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain weekly earnings totals as financial accountability metrics.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural obligation for the core financial metric entity required for disbursement accountability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view their earnings totals.
payload.rationale: Functional capability derived from How-column display function, supporting the Why-column goal of building financial awareness through earnings visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide for pocket money disbursement by parents based on reported earnings.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable the disbursement process, supporting the Why-column goal of facilitating accurate pocket money disbursement through reporting.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into available tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable informed participation by ensuring children can see task opportunities. Derived from the visibility provision function and the goal of task awareness.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into claimed tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable children to track their claimed commitments. Derived from the visibility provision function and the goal of task awareness.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into their earnings.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to support financial literacy by enabling children to view their accumulated earnings and understand the financial outcomes of completed tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain persistent records of all completed obligations.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to maintain historical records of completed obligations. All three CCIs support this obligation: the What-column identifies the historical record artifact, the How-column addresses the maintenance function, and the Why-column establishes the accountability purpose.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain persistent records of all financial transactions.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to maintain historical records of financial transactions. All three CCIs support this obligation: the What-column identifies the historical record artifact, the How-column addresses the maintenance function, and the Why-column establishes the accountability purpose.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure historical records are auditable.
payload.rationale: Constrains the quality of maintained records to be auditable, which is a governance rule on how records must be kept. Both CCIs contribute to this auditability constraint: the What-column characterizes the record as an accountability artifact and the Why-column establishes accountability as the governing principle.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records to support dispute resolution.
payload.rationale: Constrains the purpose for which historical records must be maintained—dispute resolution support. This is a governance rule expressing the Why obligation: accountability for resolving disputes requires records to be kept.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records to support institutional memory.
payload.rationale: Constrains the purpose for which historical records must be maintained—institutional memory support. This is a governance rule expressing the Why obligation: accountability for preserving institutional knowledge requires records to be kept.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's task completion.

payload.statement: The business shall govern earnings calculation within weekly period boundaries.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a parent entity with governance responsibilities for child participation.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion derived from the What-column entity (Parent). The entity represents the business role with governance responsibilities, distinct from the actor capability.

payload.statement: The business shall provide visibility into child participation patterns.
payload.rationale: Business-subject capability derived from the Why-column goal. Expresses the business responsibility to enable parental oversight of child participation.

payload.statement: The business shall provide visibility into child completion achievements.
payload.rationale: Business-subject capability derived from the Why-column goal. Expresses the business responsibility to enable parental oversight of child achievements to inform payment fulfillment.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of historical participation events.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of historical completion outcomes.

payload.statement: A child user can interact with the system to participate in chore and reward activities.

payload.statement: A child user can claim an available task.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement (boundary-crossing) expressing the child user's capability to claim tasks, directly realizing the enterprise commitment to enable children to claim tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks claimable by child users.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement (black-box affordance) expressing what capability the system provides at the boundary to enable task claiming, complementary to the actor-action statement.

payload.statement: A child user can view available tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing prerequisite capability for task claiming—children must be able to view available tasks before claiming them.

payload.statement: A child user can view completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement enabling children to view their completed tasks, supporting their participation in the task lifecycle after claiming.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise task completion events established by child users.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the responsibility to recognize and record when a child completes a claimed task, necessary for establishing entitlement to compensation.

payload.statement: A task shall comprise a monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion defining a necessary attribute of the Task entity—monetary value is required for children to understand the compensation for claiming a task.

payload.statement: A task shall comprise a completion status.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion defining a necessary attribute of the Task entity—completion status is required to distinguish available from completed tasks in the claiming lifecycle.

payload.statement: A parent user can define a task.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement (boundary-crossing) expressing the parent user's capability to define tasks, directly realizing the enterprise commitment to enable parents to establish tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make task definition capability available to parent users.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a relationship between parent and defined task.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the responsibility to record and preserve the authorship relationship between a parent and the tasks they define, supporting governance and accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly reset cycle for tasks.
payload.rationale: Constraint expressing a temporal governance rule governing task lifecycle—tasks must reset weekly at the week-end boundary, constraining how task establishment behaves across temporal periods.
payload.fit_criteria: Tasks shall reset at the weekly reset boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a Child logical entity with attributes sufficient to support task completion association.
payload.rationale: Row 2 seed recognises Child as participant; Row 3 defines the logical entity structure required to realise that participation in the task completion domain.
payload.fit_criteria: Child entity model includes identity and attributes required for completion association.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a Parent logical entity with attributes sufficient to support task definition association.
payload.rationale: Row 2 seed recognises Parent as participant; Row 3 defines the logical entity structure required to support the parent-task definition relationship.
payload.fit_criteria: Parent entity model includes identity and attributes required for task definition association.

payload.statement: The system shall transition a task to a claimable state when eligibility conditions are satisfied for a given child.
payload.rationale: Row 2 seed makes tasks claimable; Row 3 defines the logical state transition that enables claiming based on eligibility logic.
payload.fit_criteria: Task state model includes a claimable state reachable through defined eligibility conditions.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce visibility constraints such that child users have access to tasks in available state.
payload.rationale: Row 2 seed makes available tasks visible; Row 3 defines the logical access boundary constraint governing child visibility of available tasks.
payload.fit_criteria: Child logical access context includes only tasks in available state.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce visibility constraints such that child users have access to their own completed task records.
payload.rationale: Row 2 seed makes completed tasks visible; Row 3 defines the logical access boundary constraint governing child visibility of completion entities associated with that child.
payload.fit_criteria: Child logical access context includes completion entities where the child is the associated participant.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce visibility constraints such that parent users have access to task definition entities.
payload.rationale: Row 2 seed makes task definition available to parents; Row 3 defines the logical access boundary constraint governing parent visibility of task definition structures.
payload.fit_criteria: Parent logical access context includes all task definition entities.

payload.statement: The system shall derive weekly earnings totals by aggregating task completion values within weekly temporal boundaries.
payload.rationale: Row 2 seed assigns business accountability for determining earnings; Row 3 defines the logical derivation behavior (temporal aggregation) that realizes this determination within the system boundary.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings derivation logic produces a total per child per weekly period by summing completion values within that period.

value_set: Historical task records can be examined and their integrity verified.
Task availability status resets at the weekly reset boundary for all recurring tasks.
Tasks shall reset at the weekly reset boundary.
Child logical access context includes only tasks in available state.
Tasks not in available state cannot be claimed.
DELETE or UPDATE statements shall not execute if the INSERT INTO historical_task_record operation fails or is not completed.

payload.name: earnings
payload.description: The enterprise shall enable children to view earnings.

value_set: Parent users can access earnings data only for children within their governance scope.

payload.name: reward
payload.description: The enterprise shall enable parents to define rewards.

payload.name: task reward amount
payload.description: The enterprise shall establish monetary reward amounts as attributes of tasks.

payload.name: task completion status
payload.description: The enterprise shall maintain task completion status.

payload.name: reward system
payload.description: The enterprise shall establish a task-based monetary reward system enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks.

payload.name: settlement cycle
payload.description: The enterprise shall establish a weekly settlement cycle for task claiming.

value_set: Task availability state transitions occurring outside the current weekly period shall be rejected.
Earnings aggregation calculations shall include only task completions within the target weekly period.

payload.name: task claiming
payload.description: The enterprise shall establish a weekly settlement cycle for task claiming.

value_set: Tasks shall reset at each weekly reset boundary.

payload.name: pocket money disbursement
payload.description: The enterprise shall provide for pocket money disbursement by parents based on reported earnings.

payload.name: obligation record
payload.description: The enterprise shall maintain persistent records of all completed obligations.

payload.name: financial transaction record
payload.description: The enterprise shall maintain persistent records of all financial transactions.

payload.name: historical record
payload.description: The enterprise shall maintain historical records to support dispute resolution.

payload.name: dispute resolution
payload.description: The enterprise shall maintain historical records to support dispute resolution.

payload.name: earnings calculation
payload.description: The business shall maintain a record of each earnings calculation.

value_set: Earnings aggregation logic operates within discrete weekly temporal boundaries.
No earnings aggregation spans multiple weekly periods.

payload.name: completion report
payload.description: The system shall make consolidated completion reports visible to the parent.

payload.name: child participation
payload.description: The business shall maintain a parent entity with governance responsibilities for child participation.

value_set: API requests for child earnings return HTTP 403 when the requesting parent lacks governance association

payload.name: task definition
payload.description: The business shall maintain a record of historical task definitions.

value_set: Parent logical access context includes all task definition entities.
Historical task records cannot transition state or modify attributes after creation.

payload.name: participation event
payload.description: The business shall maintain a record of historical participation events.

value_set: Child logical access context includes completion entities where the child is the associated participant.
Every task completion entity instance references a Child entity.
Every task completion entity instance references a monetary value attribute.
Aggregation queries include week-start and week-end timestamp predicates

payload.name: retention requirement
payload.description: The business shall retain historical task data in accordance with the established retention requirement.

value_set: Historical task data is not removed before the retention period expires.
Historical records older than the retention period are removed within one retention cycle

payload.name: task availability state
payload.description: The system shall transition task availability state at weekly cycle boundaries.

value_set: Task availability transitions occur only at weekly cycle boundaries.

payload.description: The system shall persist Child entity records in a relational store with columns for child identifier, name, and account credentials.

payload.description: The system shall persist Parent entity records in a relational store with columns for parent identifier, name, and account credentials.

payload.name: eligibility rule
payload.description: The system shall implement a state transition service that evaluates eligibility rules and updates task status fields in the relational store.

payload.name: child role session
payload.description: The system shall implement role-based access control middleware that filters task query results to include only records with status='available' for child-role sessions.

payload.name: task identifier
payload.description: The system shall persist Task entity records in a relational store with columns for task identifier and status.

payload.name: task parent id
payload.description: The Task relational table shall include a parent_id foreign key column referencing the Parent table primary key.

payload.name: message queue
payload.description: The system shall publish task claim events to a message queue or event stream consumed by parent-facing notification components.

payload.name: notification component
payload.description: The system shall publish task claim events to a message queue or event stream consumed by parent-facing notification components.

payload.name: REST API endpoint
payload.description: The system shall expose task completion records via a REST API endpoint or service interface accessible to parent-facing application components.

payload.name: application component
payload.description: The system shall expose task completion records via a REST API endpoint or service interface accessible to parent-facing application components.

payload.name: archive table
payload.description: The system shall persist historical task records in a relational archive table or time-series data store.

payload.name: foreign key
payload.description: The system shall implement a relational foreign key or document reference linking task completion event records to child entity records.

value_set: Database rejects insert or update operations that violate the non-null foreign key constraint.

payload.name: relational database
payload.description: The system shall persist task completion event records in a relational database with temporal retention policy enforcement.

payload.name: archival service
payload.description: The system shall implement automated archival or purge services for historical task data based on configurable retention period.

payload.name: state machine component
payload.description: The system shall implement a state machine component to manage task lifecycle transitions including claimed state.

payload.name: task database schema
payload.description: The task database schema shall include a monetary value field.

payload.name: form control
payload.description: The parent user interface component shall provide form controls for task definition input and submission to the backend service.

payload.name: operating system version
payload.description: The system shall support deployment on operating system versions that meet the minimum compatibility requirements.

value_set: System installation blocked on Android API levels below 23; system executes successfully on API level 23 and higher
System operates on Windows 10 version 1809 and all subsequent versions

payload.name: compatibility requirement
payload.description: The system shall support deployment on operating system versions that meet the minimum compatibility requirements.

payload.name: week start date
payload.description: The aggregation service shall compute week_start_date as DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE) when querying task completion records.

payload.name: week end date
payload.description: The aggregation service shall compute week_end_date as week_start_date + INTERVAL '7 days' when querying task completion records.

payload.name: iOS mobile node
payload.description: The system shall deploy client application instances to iOS mobile nodes.

value_set: System installs and executes on iOS mobile platforms
System installs and executes on Android mobile platforms
System installs and executes on Windows desktop platforms

payload.name: database role
payload.description: The task_history table shall enforce read-only access by revoking UPDATE and DELETE privileges for all application database roles.

payload.surface_term: task history source task completion id

payload.name: child created at
payload.description: The child entity table shall include columns child_id (integer, primary key), name (varchar(100)), and created_at (timestamp).

payload.name: task history description
payload.description: The system shall create a task_history table with columns task_id (integer), description (varchar(500)), reward_value (decimal(10,2)), completed_at (timestamp), child_id (integer), archived_at (timestamp).

payload.name: task history archived at
payload.description: The system shall create a task_history table with columns task_id (integer), description (varchar(500)), reward_value (decimal(10,2)), completed_at (timestamp), child_id (integer), archived_at (timestamp).

payload.surface_term: task completion event completed at timestamp
task completion event child user id

payload.name: data retention service configuration
payload.description: The data retention service configuration shall include a numeric parameter 'retention_period_days' with a default value of 365 and a minimum value of 30.

payload.name: task state type
payload.description: The task state machine component shall define an enumerated state type with values: {AVAILABLE, CLAIMED, COMPLETED}.

payload.name: transition function
payload.description: The task state machine component shall implement a transition function that accepts (current_state, event) tuples and returns next_state or raises InvalidTransitionException.

payload.name: task event
payload.description: The task state machine component shall implement a transition function that accepts (current_state, event) tuples and returns next_state or raises InvalidTransitionException.

value_set: Transition function returns InvalidTransitionException when claim event applied to CLAIMED or COMPLETED state

payload.name: task name
payload.description: The parent UI component shall render HTML form controls including TEXT input for task name, TEXTAREA for description, and NUMBER input for monetary value.

payload.name: database transaction
payload.description: The system shall execute the INSERT statement within a database transaction.

payload.name: task definition recurrence pattern
payload.description: The task_definition table shall include columns for task_id (primary key, auto-increment integer), task_name (varchar(255), not null, unique), description (text, nullable), reward_amount (decimal(10,2), not null), recurrence_pattern (varchar(50), not null), and is_active (boolean, not null, default true).

payload.name: task definition is active
payload.description: The task_definition table shall include columns for task_id (primary key, auto-increment integer), task_name (varchar(255), not null, unique), description (text, nullable), reward_amount (decimal(10,2), not null), recurrence_pattern (varchar(50), not null), and is_active (boolean, not null, default true).

payload.name: task assignment
payload.description: The completed task assignment entity shall consist of a task identifier and a child actor identifier attribute pair.

payload.name: task definition operation
payload.description: The parent control interface shall provide an actor capable of performing task definition operations.

payload.name: deployment
payload.description: The system shall support deployment to an iOS mobile platform node.

payload.name: parent entity table
payload.description: The system shall implement the parent entity table with a foreign key column referencing the primary key of the child entity table to enforce the one-to-many relationship.
